title: Phone shows SOS only after SIM swap
summary: Troubleshoot a line that shows SOS only, Emergency Calls Only, or No Service immediately after moving a SIM into another handset.
symptoms: SIM works in the old phone but not the new phone
common_causes: old eSIM profile is still active
IMEI is not fully provisioned on the line
network settings are stale after the swap
resolution_steps: Confirm the handset is unlocked and supports the carrier bands.
Restart the phone, then toggle airplane mode for 30 seconds.
If the device used eSIM before, delete inactive or duplicate cellular plans.
Reset network settings and reinsert the SIM after the reset completes.
If service does not return within 15 minutes, reprovision the line and verify the IMEI on the account.
escalation_policy: Escalate to network provisioning if SOS only continues after reprovisioning and lock status has been confirmed.
related_articles: Activate eSIM on iPhone with QR code
5G icon missing even though coverage is available

title: 5G icon missing even though coverage is available
summary: Use this article when a customer expects 5G service but the device stays on LTE or 4G in an area marked as 5G coverage.
symptoms: speed tests are slower than expected
other devices nearby show 5G on the same carrier
common_causes: SIM card is too old for 5G provisioning
preferred network type is set to LTE only
local site is capacity constrained and falls back to LTE
resolution_steps: Verify the line is on a 5G-capable plan and the handset model supports the required bands.
Check the preferred network mode and change it to 5G Auto or NR/LTE.
Refresh network provisioning and confirm the SIM was issued within the last three years.
Run a coverage check for outages or site maintenance in the serving area.
If all checks pass, recommend a SIM replacement or eSIM reprovisioning.
escalation_policy: Escalate to radio access network support only after plan eligibility, handset support, and provisioning have been validated.
related_articles: Phone shows SOS only after SIM swap
International roaming enabled but data will not connect

title: Wi-Fi calling unavailable after handset upgrade
summary: Restore Wi-Fi calling after a device upgrade, especially when voice over LTE works but Wi-Fi calling cannot be enabled or registered.
symptoms: Wi-Fi Calling toggle is missing or disabled
calls drop indoors with poor cellular signal
customer gets an address verification error
common_causes: VPN or firewall blocks IPSec or IMS traffic
resolution_steps: Confirm the device model supports carrier Wi-Fi calling and has the latest OS update.
Update the emergency address on the account and have the customer accept the Wi-Fi calling terms again.
Disable VPN, connect to a different Wi-Fi network, and retry registration.
Refresh the line features, restart the phone, and test with airplane mode on plus Wi-Fi enabled.
If the toggle is still missing, reset network settings and reinstall the eSIM if present.
escalation_policy: Escalate to IMS support if Wi-Fi calling registration fails on multiple networks after address validation and feature refresh.
related_articles: 5G icon missing even though coverage is available
Phone shows SOS only after SIM swap

title: International roaming enabled but data will not connect
summary: Resolve cases where voice roaming works abroad but mobile data stays disconnected or a customer cannot attach to a partner network.
symptoms: calls work but data does not
device shows bars but no internet
manual network selection lists several roaming partners
common_causes: data roaming is disabled on the device
the roaming pass is missing or expired
APN settings were overwritten by a local SIM
the current partner network does not support LTE data for the plan
resolution_steps: Confirm international roaming and roaming data passes are active on the line.
Enable data roaming in the device settings and reboot once.
Reset APN values to the carrier defaults and remove any manually entered proxy settings.
Try manual network selection using a preferred roaming partner from the coverage tool.
If service still fails, collect location, visited network, and timestamp for roaming partner escalation.
escalation_policy: Escalate to international roaming operations when multiple partner networks fail after APN reset and pass verification.
related_articles: 5G icon missing even though coverage is available
